digest.top_line: SENOMAC: 5-yr RFS 89.7% vs 88.7% (HR 0.89) — completion ALND omittable for 1-2 macromet sentinel nodes, but nodal RT reached ~90% of both arms.
digest.tldr: Breast carried the day. SENOMAC extends Z0011/AMAROS to a macromet-only cohort including mastectomy, T3, and extracapsular extension, confirming completion ALND adds nothing at 46.8-mo f/u. RT caveat drives the read: ~90% of both arms got nodal irradiation, so this validates dropping ALND WITH regional nodal RT, not omitting axillary treatment.

sites.intro: De-escalating axillary surgery in a setting where the nodal basin is still being irradiated.

studies.name: SENOMAC
studies.tldr: 5-yr RFS 89.7% vs 88.7%, HR 0.89 (0.66-1.19): omitting completion ALND noninferior, with nodal RT given in ~90% of both arms.
details: 📊 1° EP is overall survival (not yet reported); this presents the prespecified secondary recurrence-free survival
🔍 Phase 3 noninferiority, 1:1, N=2766 enrolled (2540 per-protocol), 67 sites in 5 countries, median f/u 46.8 mo
🔍 cN0 T1-T3 with 1-2 sentinel-node macrometastases (>2mm); extends beyond Z0011 to mastectomy, T3, extracapsular extension, and men
🔍 Nodal RT (incl. nodal target volumes) reached 89.9% (SNB-only) and 88.4% (cALND): axillary basin irradiated in ~90% of both arms
🔗 vs Z0011 and AMAROS: both showed no ALND benefit at 10 yr; SENOMAC confirms in a larger macromet-only cohort with new subgroups
⚠️ RT confounds attribution: this validates dropping ALND WITH regional nodal irradiation, not omitting axillary treatment entirely
⚠️ Secondary RFS at 46.8-mo median f/u is short for ER+ biology

verdict.rationale: Randomised phase 3 noninferiority; prespecified secondary RFS met, extends Z0011/AMAROS de-escalation to mastectomy/T3/ECE/men. Primary OS not yet reported.
verdict.audience: cN0 T1-T3 breast cancer, 1-2 sentinel-node macrometastases

studies.significance: Nodal RT with nodal target volumes reached ~90% in both arms (89.9% vs 88.4%), so SENOMAC validates dropping completion ALND within regional nodal irradiation, not omitting axillary treatment. For the RT reader it positions nodal RT as the axillary treatment when SNB shows 1-2 macromets; it does not test omitting both surgery and RT.
studies.monday_clinic: In cN0 breast cancer with 1-2 sentinel-node macrometastases who receive regional nodal RT, this supports omitting completion ALND, including mastectomy, T3, extracapsular extension, and men; it does not extend to patients treated without nodal radiotherapy.

primary_endpoint.name: Recurrence-free survival (secondary)
primary_endpoint.stat_detail: 95% CI 0.66-1.19, NI met (margin 1.44); primary OS not yet reported

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Phase 3 **noninferiority** RCT, 1:1, **N=2766** enrolled (2540 per-protocol), 67 sites across 5 countries; median follow-up **46.8 mo**. Reports the prespecified **secondary RFS**; primary **OS not yet reported**.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: **cN0 T1-T3** breast cancer with **1-2 sentinel-node macrometastases** (>2mm). Extends eligibility beyond Z0011 to **mastectomy, T3, extracapsular extension, and men**.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Nodal RT including nodal target volumes reached **89.9%** (SNB-only) and **88.4%** (cALND). ALND omission was therefore tested **within regional nodal irradiation**, not as omission of axillary treatment.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary: overall survival** (not reported in source). This analysis: prespecified **secondary recurrence-free survival**, per-protocol and modified ITT.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: **Noninferiority met**: the upper confidence bound stayed below the prespecified **1.44** margin (P<0.001) across **191** recurrence-or-death events.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Consistent with **Z0011** (breast-conserving + whole-breast RT) and **AMAROS** (axillary RT replacing ALND), both null for ALND benefit at 10 yr. SENOMAC adds the **macromet-only, mastectomy/T3/ECE/male** groups in a larger cohort.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents cN0 T1-T3 breast cancer with 1-2 sentinel-node macrometastases receiving guideline adjuvant therapy and nodal RT. Does not represent clinically node-positive disease, 3+ positive nodes, or patients not receiving nodal radiotherapy.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: This is the **secondary RFS** endpoint, not the OS primary, at a **46.8-mo** median follow-up short for ER+ biology. The **HR 1.44** noninferiority margin is generous, and ~90% nodal RT confounds attribution to surgical omission alone.

open_questions: Overall survival (primary endpoint) result still pending
Can nodal RT be omitted alongside ALND in this population?
Durability beyond 5 years in ER+ disease

related_trials.brief_title: Evaluating Omitting of Internal Mammary Irradiation Among Early Stage Intermediate Risk (N1) Breast Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: The effect of internal mammary irradiation (IMI) added to whole-breast or thoracic-wall irradiation plus supraclavicular (SVC) irradiation after surgery on survival among women with early-stage intermediate risk (N1) breast cancer remains debated. The present study aimed to identified patient could be omitted from internal mammary lymph node irradiation by using a clinical-genomic model.

related_trials.brief_title: The T-REX Trial: Tailored Regional External Beam Radiotherapy in Clinically Node-negative Breast Cancer Patients With 1-2 Sentinel Node Macrometastases.
related_trials.brief_summary:
T-REX is a randomized multicenter, non-inferiority trial.

Aim: To evaluate whether regional radiotherapy may safely be omitted in clinically node negative breast cancer patients with one or two sentinel node macrometastases and an estrogen receptor positive, HER2-negative tumor. Leading to an improved quality of life and reduced side effects, without worsening recurrence free survival at five years.

Intervention: Patients will be randomized to locoregional radiotherapy towards the…
